articles.title: Iran conflict lifts mortgage rates, but housing demand stays positive
articles.summary: Pending sales rose to 63,971 versus 61,143 in 2025, inventory ended at 844,011, and price cuts were 39.57% versus 41%.

articles.title: These underperforming trades could yield big returns over next six months
articles.summary: ETF Action's Mike Akins is encouraging investors to boost exposure to groups that underperformed compared with major artificial intelligence stocks.

articles.title: Trump didn’t sign it, but the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act is now law
articles.summary: The legislation, aimed at cutting red tape and making homeownership more attainable, is now the law of the land after President Donald Trump declined to sign or veto the bill before midnight Eastern time Saturday.

articles.title: Century 21 COO says M&A activity fueled by growing tech demands
articles.summary: Century 21 has completed 16 merger and acquisition deals in 2026, following 24 transactions last year — a roughly 40% increase from 2022.

articles.title: Plaintiffs oppose Veterans United motion to dismiss amended RESPA class-action suit
articles.summary: Fifteen named plaintiffs allege that Veterans United Home Loans — the nation's largest VA mortgage lender — ran an illegal kickback and steering scheme that funneled borrowers into overpriced loans.

articles.title: Rechat’s Testimonials tool turns client praise into marketing content
articles.summary: Rechat said many agents currently rely on multiple platforms, leaving reviews scattered across websites or stored as screenshots and emails.

articles.title: American Real Estate Association warns Missouri ballot measures could raise homeownership costs
articles.summary: ARA said the two ballot measures would allow lawmakers to raise taxes while limiting the public’s ability to respond.

articles.title: Housing affordability is improving as wages outpace home-price growth
articles.summary: Inventory is 1.56 million with 4.6 months of supply, helping keep 1.8% home-price growth below wage gains running near 3.5%.

articles.title: New policy impact may ignite a manufactured housing blue-sky era
articles.summary: ROAD to Housing Act would make the permanent chassis optional, enabling new designs and potential lower-cost financing paths.

articles.title: Data centers emerge as real estate’s newest pricing wildcard

articles.title: Private chef salaries reach $300,000 as the rich seek their own Michelin stars
articles.summary: Demand for chefs, personal assistants, butlers, nannies, housekeepers, chauffeurs and estate managers have reached records, according to Morgan & Mallet.

articles.title: Prediction markets spark insider trading concerns. Here's how Goldman and other companies are responding
articles.summary: CNBC reached out to 50 companies about what are their trading policies for employees on prediction markets. A handful had an answer.

articles.title: Kalshi traders think gas prices will stay higher for longer as U.S.-Iran tensions heat back up
